# Phase 57: Data Model & Backend - Pattern Map

**Mapped:** 2026-05-26
**Files analyzed:** 5 (3 modified, 2 auto-generated)
**Analogs found:** 5 / 5

## Pattern Assignments

### `src-tauri/src/models/task.rs` — Task struct extension (DATA-01)

**Analog:** `src-tauri/src/models/task.rs` (self — extend existing Task struct)

**Existing TASK_SELECT constant** (lines 5–15):
```rust
/// SQL SELECT clause for all task columns, matching Task::from_row column order.
///
/// Column order: id(0), project_id(1), title(2), description(3), status(4), priority(5),
/// base_branch(6), archived_at(7), external_id(8), is_imported(9), import_source(10),
/// skills(11), model_override(12), mcp_allowlist(13), skills_override(14), labels(15),
/// external_url(16), external_updated_at(17), created_at(18), updated_at(19)
pub const TASK_SELECT: &str =
    "SELECT id, project_id, title, description, status, priority, \
     base_branch, archived_at, external_id, is_imported, import_source, skills, \
     model_override, mcp_allowlist, skills_override, labels, \
     external_url, external_updated_at, created_at, updated_at FROM tasks";
```

**What to change in TASK_SELECT:** Extend the column comment to include `auto_approve(20)` and `isolated_worktree(21)`, then append both columns to the SELECT list:
```rust
/// Column order: id(0), ... updated_at(19), auto_approve(20), isolated_worktree(21)
pub const TASK_SELECT: &str =
    "SELECT id, project_id, title, description, status, priority, \
     base_branch, archived_at, external_id, is_imported, import_source, skills, \
     model_override, mcp_allowlist, skills_override, labels, \
     external_url, external_updated_at, created_at, updated_at, \
     auto_approve, isolated_worktree FROM tasks";
```

**Existing Task struct** (lines 17–49) — fields to add after `updated_at`:
```rust
pub auto_approve: bool,
pub isolated_worktree: bool,
```

**Existing from_row method** (lines 116–139) — entries to add after index 19:
```rust
auto_approve: row.get::<_, bool>(20).unwrap_or(false),
isolated_worktree: row.get::<_, bool>(21).unwrap_or(true),
```
Note: rusqlite reads SQLite INTEGER 0/1 as `bool` natively via `row.get::<_, bool>(n)`. No special conversion.

**TaskAttachment model to add** (after existing TaskInstruction, lines 61–69 pattern):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, Type)]
#[specta(export)]
pub struct TaskAttachment {
    pub id: i32,
    pub task_id: i32,
    pub filename: String,
    pub file_path: String,
    pub file_size: i64,
    pub created_at: String,
}
```
Note: `file_size` must be `i64` (not `i32`) — i32 max is ~2.1 GB. Matches `file_size INTEGER NOT NULL` in schema.

**models/mod.rs re-export** — add `TaskAttachment` to the existing task re-export line (line 15):
```rust
pub use task::{Task, TaskStatus, TaskPriority, TaskRelationship, TaskInstruction, TaskAttachment, CreateTaskRequest, ProjectConfigResponse, ProjectConfigRequest, TaskConfigRequest, TASK_SELECT};
```

**lib.rs pub use line** (line 15) — add `TaskAttachment` to the `pub use models::` line.

### `src-tauri/src/db/schema.rs` — Schema V18 migration (DATA-02)

**Analog:** `src-tauri/src/db/schema.rs` (self — extend V17 to V18)

**Existing version constant** (line 3):
```rust
pub const SCHEMA_VERSION: u32 = 17;
```
Change to `18`. Rename the SQL constant from `SCHEMA_V17` to `SCHEMA_V18`. Update the `conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_V17)?;` call (line 196) to `SCHEMA_V18`.

**tasks table additions inside the CREATE TABLE DDL** — after `labels TEXT DEFAULT '[]'` (line 49), before the closing FOREIGN KEY constraint:
```sql
auto_approve INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
isolated_worktree INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
```

**task_attachments table to add** (after task_instructions, following the CASCADE FK pattern from `task_reviews` at lines 97–105 and `task_instructions` at lines 66–74):
```sql
-- Task attachments table: stores file attachment metadata for tasks
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_attachments (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    task_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    filename TEXT NOT NULL,
    file_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    file_size INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (task_id) REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_task_attachments_task_id ON task_attachments(task_id);
```

**Destructive migration drop block** (lines 179–194) — add `task_attachments` as the FIRST drop (before `session_aliases`) due to FK dependency on `tasks`:
```sql
PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS task_attachments;   -- NEW: must precede tasks
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS session_aliases;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS review_comments;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS task_reviews;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS task_instructions;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS task_relationships;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS worktrees;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS tasks;
-- ... rest unchanged
PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
```

**Schema test updates** (lines 206–283) — add assertions matching the existing pattern:
```rust
// In the tables list assertion block:
assert!(tables.contains(&"task_attachments".to_string()));

// In the task columns assertion block (after existing assertions for labels, external_url, etc.):
assert!(task_columns.contains(&"auto_approve".to_string()));
assert!(task_columns.contains(&"isolated_worktree".to_string()));

// Update the hardcoded version assertion (line 251):
assert_eq!(version, 18);  // was 17
```

### `src-tauri/src/ipc/task_handlers.rs` — Attachment CRUD (DATA-03)

**Analog:** `src-tauri/src/ipc/task_handlers.rs` — `get_task_instructions` / `add_task_instruction` / `remove_task_relationship` patterns (lines 286–337, 252–283)

**Imports pattern** (lines 1–5 — no changes needed, `TaskAttachment` will be available via `crate::models` once added to mod.rs):
```rust
use std::sync::Arc;
use tauri::{Emitter, State};
use chrono::Utc;
use crate::models::{Task, TaskRelationship, TaskInstruction, TaskAttachment, TASK_SELECT};
use crate::db::AppState;
```

**get_task_attachments — follows get_task_instructions pattern** (lines 286–315):
```rust
#[tauri::command]
#[specta::specta]
pub fn get_task_attachments(
    app_state: State<Arc<AppState>>,
    task_id: i32,
) -> Result<Vec<TaskAttachment>, String> {
    let conn = app_state.db.lock().map_err(|e| format!("Lock failed: {}", e))?;
    let mut stmt = conn
        .prepare(
            "SELECT id, task_id, filename, file_path, file_size, created_at \
             FROM task_attachments WHERE task_id = ? ORDER BY created_at ASC",
        )
        .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;

    let rows = stmt
        .query_map([task_id], |row| {
            Ok(TaskAttachment {
                id: row.get(0)?,
                task_id: row.get(1)?,
                filename: row.get(2)?,
                file_path: row.get(3)?,
                file_size: row.get(4)?,
                created_at: row.get(5)?,
            })
        })
        .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?
        .collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()
        .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;

    Ok(rows)
}
```

**add_task_attachment — follows add_task_instruction pattern** (lines 318–337):
```rust
#[tauri::command]
#[specta::specta]
pub fn add_task_attachment(
    app_state: State<Arc<AppState>>,
    task_id: i32,
    filename: String,
    file_path: String,
    file_size: i64,
) -> Result<TaskAttachment, String> {
    let conn = app_state.db.lock().map_err(|e| format!("Lock failed: {}", e))?;
    let now = Utc::now().to_rfc3339();
    conn.execute(
        "INSERT INTO task_attachments (task_id, filename, file_path, file_size, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
        rusqlite::params![task_id, &filename, &file_path, file_size, &now],
    )
    .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;

    let id = conn.last_insert_rowid() as i32;
    Ok(TaskAttachment { id, task_id, filename, file_path, file_size, created_at: now })
}
```

**remove_task_attachment — follows remove_task_relationship pattern** (lines 276–283):
```rust
#[tauri::command]
#[specta::specta]
pub fn remove_task_attachment(
    app_state: State<Arc<AppState>>,
    attachment_id: i32,
) -> Result<(), String> {
    let conn = app_state.db.lock().map_err(|e| format!("Lock failed: {}", e))?;
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM task_attachments WHERE id = ?", [attachment_id])
        .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
    Ok(())
}
```

Note: All three attachment handlers are synchronous (`fn`, not `async fn`) — they only touch the sync DB mutex, matching the pattern of all existing task CRUD handlers.

### `src-tauri/src/ipc/task_handlers.rs` — interrupt_task (DATA-04)

**Analogs:**
- ACP cancellation: `src-tauri/src/ipc/acp_handlers.rs` `cancel_acp_session` (lines 424–463)
- PTY close: `src-tauri/src/ipc/execution_handlers.rs` `close_pty_session` (lines 539–564)
- DB status update: `src-tauri/src/ipc/task_handlers.rs` `archive_task` (lines 186–207)

**cancel_acp_session core pattern** (acp_handlers.rs lines 424–463):
```rust
pub async fn cancel_acp_session(
    app_state: State<'_, Arc<AppState>>,
    log_id: i32,
) -> Result<(), String> {
    // Send CancelRequest best-effort
    let session_id = session_id_for(log_id);
    let cancel_msg = MaestroRpcMessage::Request(ServerRequest::Cancel(CancelRequest { session_id }));
    let _ = crate::acp::write_to_acp_session(&app_state, log_id, &cancel_msg).await;

    // Remove from acp_sessions — drops Child (kill_on_drop)
    let teardown_key: Option<ConnectionKey> = {
        let mut sessions = app_state.acp.sessions.lock().await;
        let removed = sessions.remove(&log_id);
        let conn_key = removed.as_ref()
            .filter(|s| s.child.is_none())
            .map(|s| s.connection_key);
        if let Some(mut session) = removed {
            if let Some(cancel_tx) = session.reader_cancel_tx.take() {
                let _ = cancel_tx.send(());
            }
        }
        conn_key
            .filter(|k| !sessions.values().any(|s| &s.connection_key == k && s.child.is_none()))
    };
    if let Some(key) = teardown_key {
        app_state.acp.connection_servers.lock().await.remove(&key);
    }
    app_state.app_handle.emit("sessions-changed", ()).ok();
    Ok(())
}
```

**close_pty_session core pattern** (execution_handlers.rs lines 539–564):
```rust
pub async fn close_pty_session(
    app_state: State<'_, Arc<AppState>>,
    session_key: i32,
) -> Result<(), String> {
    // Cancel any active attach reader
    {
        let mut cancel_map = app_state.pty.attach_cancel.lock().await;
        if let Some(flag) = cancel_map.remove(&session_key) {
            flag.store(true, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);
        }
    }
    // Remove local PTY session (dropping PtySession kills child process)
    app_state.pty.sessions.lock().await.remove(&session_key);
    // Remove remote SSH PTY session (dropping SshPtyHandle closes write channel)
    app_state.ssh.pty_sessions.lock().await.remove(&session_key);
    // Remove session metadata so get_active_sessions no longer lists it
    app_state.pty.session_meta.lock().await.remove(&session_key);
    app_state.app_handle.emit("sessions-changed", ()).ok();
    Ok(())
}
```

**interrupt_task full pattern** — async (needs tokio locks), sync DB mutex must not be held across await points:
```rust
#[tauri::command]
#[specta::specta]
pub async fn interrupt_task(
    app_state: State<'_, Arc<AppState>>,
    task_id: i32,
) -> Result<(), String> {
    // Search ACP sessions for task_id match — lock scope ends before any further await
    let acp_log_id: Option<i32> = {
        let sessions = app_state.acp.sessions.lock().await;
        sessions.iter()
            .find(|(_, proc)| proc.task_id == Some(task_id))
            .map(|(key, _)| *key)
    };

    // Search PTY session_meta for task_id match (covers both local and SSH PTY)
    let pty_log_id: Option<i32> = {
        let meta = app_state.pty.session_meta.lock().await;
        meta.iter()
            .find(|(_, m)| m.task_id == Some(task_id))
            .map(|(key, _)| *key)
    };

    if acp_log_id.is_none() && pty_log_id.is_none() {
        return Err(format!("No active session for task {}", task_id));
    }

    // Stop ACP session — mirror cancel_acp_session logic directly
    if let Some(log_id) = acp_log_id {
        // ... (replicate cancel_acp_session core body from acp_handlers.rs lines 431–459) ...
    }

    // Stop PTY session — mirror close_pty_session logic directly
    if let Some(log_id) = pty_log_id {
        // ... (replicate close_pty_session core body from execution_handlers.rs lines 546–560) ...
    }

    // Acquire sync DB mutex AFTER all async work — must not be held across await
    let now = chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339();
    {
        let conn = app_state.db.lock().map_err(|e| format!("Lock failed: {}", e))?;
        conn.execute(
            "UPDATE tasks SET status = 'Backlog', updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?",
            rusqlite::params![&now, task_id],
        ).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
    }
    app_state.app_handle.emit("tasks-changed", ()).ok();
    app_state.app_handle.emit("sessions-changed", ()).ok();
    Ok(())
}
```

Critical: The sync `app_state.db.lock()` (std::sync::Mutex) must be acquired in its own block after all tokio async locks are released. Violating this causes compile warnings about MutexGuard held across await and potential deadlock.

### `src/types/bindings.ts` — Auto-generated

**Analog:** N/A — generated by `pnpm tauri:gen` (`cargo test generate_typescript_bindings`).

No manual edits. After all Rust changes are complete, run:
```bash
pnpm tauri:gen
```

This regenerates `src/types/bindings.ts` with the new `TaskAttachment` type, updated `Task` type (with `auto_approve: boolean` and `isolated_worktree: boolean`), and the four new IPC function signatures.

## Shared Patterns

### DB Mutex Locking
**Source:** `src-tauri/src/ipc/task_handlers.rs` lines 14, 73, 193
**Apply to:** All three attachment CRUD handlers and the sync DB block in `interrupt_task`
```rust
let conn = app_state.db.lock().map_err(|e| format!("Lock failed: {}", e))?;
```

### Timestamp Generation
**Source:** `src-tauri/src/ipc/task_handlers.rs` line 45
**Apply to:** `add_task_attachment` and `interrupt_task`
```rust
let now = Utc::now().to_rfc3339();
```

### Event Emission
**Source:** `src-tauri/src/ipc/task_handlers.rs` lines 75, 148, 205
**Apply to:** `interrupt_task` (two events), `add_task_attachment` (optional — none emitted by `add_task_instruction`)
```rust
app_state.app_handle.emit("tasks-changed", ()).ok();
app_state.app_handle.emit("sessions-changed", ()).ok();
```
Note: `.ok()` is intentional here — event emission failure is non-fatal and should not propagate. This is the one exception to the "never silently discard errors" rule, established by the existing pattern.

### IPC Command Signature
**Source:** `src-tauri/src/ipc/task_handlers.rs` lines 8–26 (synchronous) and `acp_handlers.rs` lines 424–429 (async)
**Apply to:** All new handlers
```rust
// Synchronous (DB-only):
#[tauri::command]
#[specta::specta]
pub fn handler_name(
    app_state: State<Arc<AppState>>,
    param: Type,
) -> Result<ReturnType, String> { ... }

// Asynchronous (tokio locks needed):
#[tauri::command]
#[specta::specta]
pub async fn handler_name(
    app_state: State<'_, Arc<AppState>>,
    param: Type,
) -> Result<ReturnType, String> { ... }
```
Note: async handlers use `State<'_, Arc<AppState>>` with explicit lifetime; sync handlers use `State<Arc<AppState>>`.

### last_insert_rowid Return Pattern
**Source:** `src-tauri/src/ipc/task_handlers.rs` lines 269–270, 335–336
**Apply to:** `add_task_attachment` (return the created struct without re-querying)
```rust
let id = conn.last_insert_rowid() as i32;
Ok(TaskAttachment { id, task_id, filename, file_path, file_size, created_at: now })
```

### Model Struct Derive Pattern
**Source:** `src-tauri/src/models/task.rs` lines 61–68 (TaskInstruction)
**Apply to:** `TaskAttachment` struct definition
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, Type)]
#[specta(export)]
pub struct TaskInstruction {
    pub id: i32,
    pub task_id: i32,
    // ...
}
```
